name: Bahrain
summary: Hosts US Navy 5th Fleet HQ. Escalating Iranian strikes now targeting TELECOM/CLOUD infrastructure: IRGC missiles hit Batelco facility in Hamala hosting AWS servers on April 1 — servers 'partly inoperative.' NOT first AWS strike (Iran hit AWS in UAE and Bahrain in early March) but first under new '18 companies' threat framework. Bapco force majeure. ALBA smelter struck. Targeting doctrine expanding: military -> energy -> industrial -> telecom/cloud.

signals.content: Iranian strikes targeted US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama and Crowne Plaza Hotel.
Bahrain's Bapco declared force majeure after waves of Iranian strikes targeted energy installations. 3 people killed.

Air raid sirens activated across Bahrain early Sunday March 29. Bahrain Interior Ministry urged citizens to take shelter and 'remain calm.' Simultaneous sirens across Kuwait, Bahrain, and Israel — broadest regional bombardment pattern of the conflict. Consistent with CSIS assessment that Iran has abandoned calibrated retaliation for 'unbridled escalation.' Bushehr nuclear plant (across the Persian Gulf from Bahrain) struck for third time in 10 days — Kuwait issued warning about potential radioactive leak risk from Bushehr. (Sources: Middle East Eye, Bahrain Interior Ministry, CNN)

IRGC struck ALBA aluminum plant (world's largest smelter) — 2 mildly injured, damage assessed. 20 missiles and 23 UAVs intercepted March 28, with damage to Bapco energy facilities. ALBA already shut 3 of 6 smelting lines (19% capacity) due to Hormuz disruption. New targeting category: industrial infrastructure with alleged US military supply chain links. (Sources: CNBC, JPost, The National, Al Arabiya, Al Jazeera)

IRGC missiles hit Batelco telecommunications facility in Hamala, Bahrain on April 1 — the facility hosts Amazon Web Services infrastructure. AWS servers rendered 'partly inoperative.' Bahrain Interior Ministry confirmed civil defense teams responded to fire at the site. This is NOT the first AWS strike — Iran previously hit AWS data centers in UAE and Bahrain in early March — but is the first strike under the new '18 companies' threat framework announced by IRGC. Targeting doctrine continues expanding: military -> energy -> industrial -> telecom/cloud infrastructure. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Free Press Journal, Gizmodo, Daily Sabah, Bahrain Interior Ministry)

edges.context: Targeted because it hosts US 5th Fleet. IRGC missiles hit Batelco/AWS facility April 1 under '18 companies' framework. Targeting expanding from military/energy/industrial to telecom/cloud infrastructure. 3 killed earlier. Air raid sirens March 29.
US Navy 5th Fleet HQ in Manama. Critical for US naval operations in Gulf. AWS infrastructure also targeted — US tech companies now in crosshairs.
